resposta:
solution: 
```python
from collections import Counter
from typing import List

class Solution:
    def uncommonFromSentences(self, s1: str, s2: str) -> List[str]:
        words = s1.split() + s2.split()
        freq = Counter(words)
        return [w for w in freq if freq[w] == 1]
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N + M)
space complexity: O(N + M)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Count every word across both sentences; uncommon words are those with frequency exactly 1. Single pass through each sentence plus a linear scan of the frequency map yields optimal asymptotics and minimal memory footprint.
